title: Season 1: The Collapse Hunt
tagline: 680 worlds tested. Zero collapses. Can you break it?

posts.title: Genesis Protocol: Open Stress Suite. Try to Break It.
posts.purpose: Establish the challenge. Create the scoreboard.

posts.title: In Crisis, Hoarding Fails. Deployment Wins.

posts.content: Week 1 update: Added resource depletion crossover data. 4 carrying capacity tiers (cap=25 to cap=200), 600 worlds. Still zero collapses. Entropy coefficient is the strongest degradation vector so far — but even at maximum burn, the organism adapts. Next target: mutation rate locked at zero. Can it survive without adaptation?

posts.title: What Happens When You Forbid Evolution?
posts.purpose: New stress vector: mutation_rate = 0. Test adaptation dependency.
posts.experiment: Lock mutation at zero, sweep catastrophe rates, 140 worlds. Does the organism collapse without the ability to adapt?

posts.content: Scoreboard update with new mutation-lock results. Updated collapse attempt count.

posts.title: We Found the Breaking Point. It Wasn't Where We Expected.
posts.purpose: Reveal the first collapse vector (if found). If not found, the story becomes 'still unbroken after N worlds.'
posts.experiment: Combine vectors: zero mutation + high catastrophe + compressed pop cap + zero treasury skim. Multi-axis stress.

posts.content: Updated scoreboard. Operator mentions if anyone has replicated.

posts.title: Season 1 Results: X Worlds. Y Collapses. Here's What Breaks a Civilization.
posts.content_notes: Total worlds across all experiments. Collapse/no-collapse summary. Strongest vector identified. Weakest vector. Lesson: what actually kills civilizations (or doesn't). Tease Season 2: 'Next season we test multi-organism interactions.'

identity.signature_line: Single-operator so far. Replication welcome. If you run it, I'll verify your hash.

rules: Lead with bold claim or question — never 'Experiment Report:'
End with invitation or challenge — never close the door
Reply to own threads within 48h — silence kills threads
Convert tables to inline format for compatibility
Link to GitHub for full data — posts are hooks, not reports
Sign off with replication invitation, not independence statement
